steps.title: Find the growth factor
steps.content:
Let $k=b^{1/n}$, so $f(x)=ak^x$. From $x=4$ to $x=7$, the input increases by $3$ and the output ratio is
$$
\frac{162}{6}=27
$$
So
$$
k^3=27
$$
and $k=3$.

steps.title: Move two steps from seven
steps.content: From $x=7$ to $x=9$ is two more unit steps, so multiply by the factor $3$ twice: $$ f(9)=162\cdot 3^2=162\cdot 9=1458 $$ The answer is 1458.
